text:
The reputation of this spot often overwhelms it.  Even though most times you will have to wait in a frantic sidewalk crowd, we managed to bypass the chaos during a quiet Monday service.
While the bowl was fundamentally sound, the chashu fell somewhat short of the gold standard. The spicy ramen was not even slightly spicy.
What truly elevates the experience is the customization; the diverse selection of extra aromatics provides a necessary depth that the kitchen’s initial seasoning only hints at...
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

Wir hoffen, dich bald wieder auf eine weitere Schüssel Ramen bei uns begrüssen zu dürfen!

text: A branch of a Yokohama based Ramen chain, so 100% authentic. Amazing! And the prices are fair as well.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Markus,

thank you very much for your fantastic review and for taking the time to share your experience with us! We are truly happy to hear that you enjoyed the authenticity of our Yokohama-style ramen, as well as the food, service, and overall atmosphere. That means a lot to our whole team.

We also appreciate your honest feedback regarding the noise level and waiting time. During busy hours our shop can get quite lively, which reflects the energetic ramen shop culture in Japan, but we are continuously working on improving our flow to reduce waiting times and make the experience more comfortable for everyone.

We hope to welcome you back again soon for another bowl of ramen!

Your Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Ich kenne jedes Ramen Restaurant in Zürich und habe auch jahrelang in Japan gelebt.

Das Machida Shoten hat mich leider sehr enttäuscht. Das Personal ist unfreundlich und es herrscht absolutes Chaos und keine der Vorspeisen ist selbst gemacht!

Wir waren am Nachmittag um 15 Uhr fast die einzigen Gäste, dennoch haben wir sehr lange auf das Essen gewartet. Wir haben alle Vorspeisen bestellt, jedoch kamen diese alle erst nachdem wir die Ramen fertig gegessen haben. Keine Entschuldigung dafür. Vor allem hat mich aber gestört, dass keine einzige Vorspeise hausgemacht war!!! Die Gyozas sind diejenigen von der Marke "Ajinomoto", welche man für den Preis eine ganze Packung im Asia Store erhält und die Takoyaki sind in der Fritteuse frittiert und ebenfalls eingekauft. Das Personal gibt aber Auskunft, dass alle Vorspeisen hausgemacht sind. Eine Frechheit!!!

Tori Paitan Ramen war bei uns viel zu salzig, Yokohama Style Ramen war ok, aber bei beiden waren sie sehr geizig mit dem Topping und die Nudeln haben geklebt. Auf Nachfrage hin, sagte das Personal, die Nudeln müssten so sein?! Generell sind die Portionen SEHR klein (etwa die Hälfte von Sappo Ramen und Ooki) und ACHTUNG Fleisch vom Ausland!
textTranslated:
I know every ramen restaurant in Zurich and also lived in Japan for many years.

Unfortunately, Machida Shoten was a huge disappointment. The staff is unfriendly, the place is in complete chaos, and none of the appetizers are homemade!

We were practically the only customers at 3 pm, yet we still waited an incredibly long time for our food. We ordered all the appetizers, but they all arrived after we had finished our ramen. No apology for that. What bothered me most, however, was that not a single appetizer was homemade! The gyozas are the kind you can buy a whole pack of at an Asian grocery store for the same price, and the takoyaki are deep-fried and also store-bought. The staff, however, insist that all the appetizers are homemade. Outrageous!

The Tori Paitan Ramen was far too salty, the Yokohama Style Ramen was okay, but they were very stingy with the toppings on both, and the noodles were sticky. When asked, the staff said the noodles were supposed to be like that?! In general, the portions are VERY small (about half the size of Sappo Ramen and Ooki) and BEWARE, the meat is imported!
responseFromOwnerText:
Hallo Herr Tran,

vielen Dank für Ihren Besuch und für das ausführliche Feedback. Es tut uns leid, dass wir Sie enttäuscht haben. Damit wir den Ablauf exakt prüfen können, teilen Sie uns bitte kurz das Datum und Uhrzeit Ihres Besuchs per Mail oder hier als zusätzlich Antwort mit – so können wir den Service an diesem Slot gezielt nachverfolgen. Dass unser Team unfreundlich wirkte, tut uns besonders leid; mit Details können wir gezielt coachen.

Snacks: Karaage und Cabbage-Salat bereiten wir frisch im Haus zu. Gyoza und Takoyaki beziehen wir – wie viele Ramen-Shops – von spezialisierten Produzenten (TK) und bereiten sie bei uns zu. Dass vor Ort der Eindruck entstand, alle Snacks seien hausgemacht, war nicht korrekt; das Team wurde bereits nachgeschult.

Reihenfolge der Speisen: Dass die Snacks nach den Ramen kamen, hätte um diese Tageszeit nicht passieren dürfen. Wir haben den Ablauf angepasst: In ruhigeren Zeiten werden Snacks vor den Ramen priorisiert; das Expo-Team wurde entsprechend gebrieft.

Ramen: Unsere Brühe ist bewusst intensiv und salzig – so ist der Machida-Stil. Für Gäste, denen das zu kräftig ist, weisen wir neuerdings an jedem Tisch darauf hin, dass man kostenlos zusätzliche Brühe bestellen kann, um die Salzigkeit zu mildern.

Zum Vergleich: Wir haben großen Respekt für Ooki und Sappo und die Produkte, die sie anbieten. Wir verfolgen jedoch leicht unterschiedliche Ansätze/Konzepte, weshalb ein 1:1-Vergleich nicht immer passt – Ihre Kritik nehmen wir dennoch sehr ernst.

Gern würden wir Sie noch einmal überzeugen. Melden Sie sich bitte kurz mit Ihren Besuchsdaten – wir kümmern uns persönlich.
Herzliche Grüsse
Ihr Machida Shoten Team Zürich
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

reviewContext.Vegetarian options: gibt eine vegane Ramen, die sehr gut ist

text:
Went back today with friends because I liked the authentic ramen last time. We arrived at 11:10 to avoid line. One unfriendly staff came out and said: „It’s too early. Go have a walk and come back later“ Don’t understand why he tried to send us away. They don’t provide a list to leave your name to secure the place. Several minutes later already a lot of other clients waiting behind us. However they were not encouraged by the staff to go for a walk. If we have listend to the staff and had gone for a walk, we would have lost our place.

When we finished eating he came and said: „want to pay?“ and presented the machine directly to us, without showing or confirmed us what we had ordered and what we ate. Why don’t provide your customers the bill? We didn’t even have the time to check if everything was correct. We were not prepared to pay directly and maybe we needed the time to discuss how we want to share the costs between us and how to pay.

And after payment not even asking if we need a receipt. Very unprofessional. I’m really annoyed by the service of that staff and it ruined our dining experience. I hope you could improve and keep the Japanese service spirit towards your customers even if it’s opened in Switzerland.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi D H

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback and for visiting us again. We are truly sorry to read that this visit left you with such a negative impression, especially after you previously enjoyed the ramen.

Regarding the situation before opening, this appears to have been a misunderstanding. As the weather has recently been quite cold and windy, our staff member intended to suggest spending the approximately 20-minute waiting time walking around the neighbourhood rather than standing outside in the cold. It was absolutely not his intention to send you away, and we are sorry if it came across that way.

Concerning the payment situation, this was clearly a mistake on our side. Our team member was under the impression that you had requested the bill and therefore approached your table directly with the payment terminal. We sincerely apologise for this misunderstanding.

You are also completely right regarding reviewing the bill together and offering a receipt. Even in times of digital payment, this remains an important part of good service. Your feedback shows us that we need to pay even closer attention to this point with our team.

We take your comments seriously and will address them internally. If you are open to it, we would appreciate the chance to welcome you again and provide the experience you expected.

Your Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Ich war kürzlich in diesem Restaurant und bin absolut begeistert! Wer auf der Suche nach traditioneller japanischer Küche in Zürich ist, kommt hier voll auf seine Kosten.
Was mir besonders gefallen hat: Das Essen die Ramen waren fantastisch die Brühe war geschmackvoll und die Nudeln hatten die perfekte Konsistenz. Man merkt sofort, dass hier Wert auf Authentizität gelegt wird. Das Ambiente die Einrichtung ist super gemütlich und versetzt einen direkt nach Japan. Der Service sobald man am Tisch sitzt, geht es extrem schnell. Wir mussten kaum auf unser Essen warten.

Ein kleiner Hinweis:
Das Restaurant ist völlig zurecht sehr beliebt. Wenn der Laden voll ist, gibt es im Inneren kaum Platz zum Stehen, weshalb man draussen warten muss. Wir haben etwa 10 bis 20 Minuten in der Schlange verbracht. Da es sich für dieses Essen aber absolut lohnt, nimmt man das gerne in Kauf.

Fazit: Wer authentische Ramen ohne grossen Schnickschnack sucht, ist hier genau richtig. Ich komme definitiv wieder!
textTranslated:
I recently visited this restaurant and I'm absolutely thrilled! Anyone looking for traditional Japanese cuisine in Zurich will be in heaven.

What I especially loved: The food—the ramen was fantastic, the broth was flavorful, and the noodles had the perfect texture. You can immediately tell that authenticity is a priority here. The ambiance and decor are incredibly cozy and transport you straight to Japan. The service is extremely fast once you're seated at your table. We barely had to wait for our food.

A quick note:

This restaurant is very popular, and rightly so. When it's packed, there's hardly any room to stand inside, so you have to wait outside. We spent about 10 to 20 minutes in line. But since the food is absolutely worth it, it's well worth the wait.

In short: If you're looking for authentic ramen without any unnecessary frills, this is the place for you. I'll definitely be back!
responseFromOwnerText:
Lieber Janic

Vielen Dank für deine ausführliche und tolle Bewertung. Es freut uns sehr zu lesen, dass du dich bei uns wohlgefühlt hast und unsere Ramen sowie die Atmosphäre so positiv erlebt hast. Besonders schön ist es für uns zu hören, dass die Authentizität und das Konzept bei dir angekommen sind.

Danke auch für deinen Hinweis zur Wartesituation. Da wir bewusst ohne Reservationen arbeiten, kann es zu Stosszeiten tatsächlich zu Wartezeiten kommen. Umso mehr freut es uns, dass du diese in Kauf genommen hast und dein Besuch sich für dich gelohnt hat. Zum Glück wird es nun langsam wieder wärmer, was das Warten draussen hoffentlich für alle Gäste etwas angenehmer macht.

Wir freuen uns schon sehr, dich bald wieder bei uns begrüssen zu dürfen. Auf die nächste Schüssel Ramen!

Dein Machida Shoten Team

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
When ramen is good, it is GOOD! This place does it well. I really enjoyed the Yokohama Max. Great broth, well cooked noodles, flavorful pork and eggs. 👍

I wouldn’t necessarily recommend going alone at this one though. Unfortunately, because they were packed to the brim (and are quite regularly as I’ve read), they seemed to view me as a waste of space and money. I never complain about this sort of thing because what I really care about is enjoying good food (hence the four stars still). I waited my turn in line for a while to get in and then got turned away at the door three times in favor of parties of 2-4 (there are plenty of tiny tables in there). I started to feel kind of humiliated at the third time until I was let in seemingly reluctantly. The couple who came in after me sat down next to me and commented, “geez, discrimination” lol. Anyway, you’ve been warned 😂.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Luke

Thank you very much for your visit and for taking the time to share such honest and detailed feedback. We are truly happy to hear that you enjoyed the Yokohama Max and appreciated the quality of the ramen. This means a lot to our team.

At the same time, we want to sincerely apologise for how your visit began. What you describe should never happen and is in fact the opposite of how we intend to operate. Especially during busy periods, solo guests are normally prioritised, as they help us fill available seats efficiently. Particularly at our large community table in the centre. No guest visiting alone should ever feel unwelcome or overlooked.

Please be assured that making every guest feel comfortable, welcome and valued is extremely important to us, even within the fast-paced ramen service we practise. We take your feedback very seriously and will address this situation internally with our team to ensure this does not happen again.

We are truly sorry that your experience started this way, and we hope you will give us another opportunity to welcome you back under better circumstances.

Dein Machida Shoten Team

reviewContext.Noise level: Loud, but you can still talk

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: They offer gluten free noodles but they use regular soy sauce that's not gluten free. What joke is this? This is extremely dangerous.
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We’re very sorry for the confusion and concern caused.

While we do offer gluten-free noodles as an alternative, we do not currently offer a fully gluten-free ramen. Our broths and seasonings contain soy sauce, which means our menu is unfortunately not suitable for guests with celiac disease.

This should have been clearly and proactively communicated by our team, and we sincerely apologise if this was not the case during your visit. We have since reinforced this information with all team members to ensure that dietary restrictions, especially those related to allergies and intolerances, are explained accurately and transparently at all times.

We completely understand how serious this matter is and appreciate you highlighting it, as it helps us improve our communication and guest safety going forward.

Kind regards,
Machida Shoten Zurich Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
This place completely blew me away. Hands down the best ramen I’ve ever had in Europe — rich, creamy, deeply eggy, and full-bodied with layers of flavour I honestly never experienced before. The noodles were absolutely perfect, the marinated egg was outstanding, and the quail egg was a delicious bonus that elevated everything even more.

Yes, we waited almost 30 minutes — and it was 100% worth it. Not even a question.

The yuzu beer paired beautifully with the ramen, refreshing and perfectly balanced. The takoyaki were fantastic, crispy and flavourful, and the black sesame ice cream was the perfect finish — bold, nutty, and not too sweet.

What truly completes the experience is the staff: incredibly friendly, attentive, and welcoming. I loved how they greet everyone who enters and warmly say goodbye in Japanese when you leave — it makes the whole place feel special and genuine.

The atmosphere is cozy, beautifully decorated, and inviting. On top of that, offering a complimentary warm drink while waiting is such a thoughtful touch and shows how much they care about their guests.

An absolute gem. I’ll be thinking about this ramen for a long time — and I’ll definitely be back.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Aleksandra

Thank you so much for this wonderful and beautifully written review. Reading your description truly made our day.

We are incredibly happy to hear that the ramen left such a strong impression on you and that you enjoyed not only the flavours but the entire experience with us. It especially means a lot to us that you noticed and appreciated the small details, like the warm tea while waiting on a cold day. These little gestures are very important to our team, and knowing they made you feel cared for is the best compliment we could receive.

Thank you again for your kind words and for sharing your experience with such warmth. We cannot wait to welcome you back for another bowl soon.

Your Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: ich war ende August 25 dort und hatte nach dem Restaurant-Besuch "China-Restaurant-Syndrom"-ähnliche Beschwerden - Bauchschmerzen und sogar leichte Atemnot. Ich weiss nicht, ob es an dem Glutamat liegt - aber diese asiatischen Süppchen mit überprozessierten Zutaten/Gewürzen zu lächerlichen Preisen (30+ Franken für ne Suppe, deren Wert nicht mehr als 5.- ist) spar ich mir in Zukunft und mache sie wenn dann selber zu Hause.
textTranslated: I was there at the end of August 2025 and after eating at the restaurant I had symptoms similar to "Chinese restaurant syndrome"—stomach pain and even mild shortness of breath. I don't know if it's due to the MSG, but I'll be skipping those Asian soups with overprocessed ingredients/spices at ridiculous prices (30+ francs for a soup worth no more than 5 francs) in the future and will make them myself at home if I do.

text: Wenn ihr es nicht hinkriegt via Uber Eats zu liefern, dann lasst es einfach. Ich machte eine Vorbestellung, wurde bestätigt; Lieferung kommt um 20:45 und um 20:20 wird die Bestellung trotz Bestätigung storniert. So etwas geht gar nicht!!
textTranslated: If you can't manage to deliver via Uber Eats, then just don't offer it. I placed a pre-order, it was confirmed; delivery was scheduled for 8:45 PM, and then at 8:20 PM the order was canceled despite the confirmation. That's completely unacceptable!
responseFromOwnerText:
Hallo Gilles,

vielen Dank für dein Feedback. Es tut uns sehr leid, dass deine Vorbestellung so kurzfristig storniert wurde – das entspricht nicht unserem Anspruch an Service.

An diesem Abend kam es zu einem unerwartet hohen Gästeaufkommen im Restaurant, weshalb wir den Lieferdienst kurzfristig pausieren mussten, um weiterhin eine gute Qualität gewährleisten zu können. Leider wurde deine Vorbestellung in diesem Moment übersehen und dadurch beim Stopp automatisch storniert.

Wir verstehen deinen Ärger vollkommen und entschuldigen uns aufrichtig für diese Situation. Gerne möchten wir dich bei einer nächsten Bestellung oder bei einem Besuch bei uns entschädigen – melde dich dafür bitte direkt unter zurich@mail.ch.machidashoten.com bei uns.

Dein Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: The food was tasty and overall enjoyable, but there are definitely better ramen restaurants in Zurich. I’m not entirely convinced by the quality or depth of the broth, and the concept feels more focused on quantity than on refinement. That said, the meal was still good and perfectly fine if you’re looking for a solid, filling ramen.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
My quest for the perfect bowl of noodle soup began on the third day of my trip to Zurich. Eager to satisfy my craving, I discovered a place called Yume Ramen, just a 14-minute walk from my hotel. However, a quick check on Google Maps warned me that the eatery might close soon, as their website indicated they shut down at 2 PM. When I arrived at 1:35 PM, my heart sank at the sight of the "CLOSED" sign hanging on the door. 😢 As someone accustomed to the vibrant dining culture of Asia, it was hard to wrap my head around a restaurant closing its doors so early.

Refusing to let disappointment deter me, I quickly began my search for another ramen spot. My eyes landed on the enticing ramen photos from Machida Shoten, but my excitement was tempered by the thought of a 47-minute walk or a 21-minute tram ride to get there. Normally, I would have opted for something closer, but something pushed me to embrace the adventure and venture out to this ramen restaurant that looked so promising online.

Upon arrival, I was warmly welcomed by the entire team, which instantly lifted my spirits. With my heart set on their signature ramen, I also decided to add some spicy minced chicken and spinach for an extra kick. The dish did not disappoint! The noodles had that delightful chewy texture I adore, and while the pork slices were slightly tougher than I would have liked, they still complemented the dish well.

To my surprise, I found myself savoring every last drop of the rich and flavorful broth—I couldn't believe I finished it all! By the end of my meal, I felt utterly satisfied and content. This culinary adventure not only fulfilled my craving but also left me with a memorable experience that I wouldn’t trade for anything. If you're in Zurich and on the hunt for delicious ramen, I wholeheartedly recommend giving Machida Shoten a try!
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Vincybie

Thank you so much for this wonderful and beautifully written review. We truly enjoyed reading about your ramen journey through Zurich!

We are especially happy that your search for the perfect bowl led you to Machida Shoten and that our team could turn the initial disappointment into a memorable experience. Hearing that you savoured every last drop of the broth is one of the greatest compliments our kitchen can receive.

Thank you as well for mentioning both the highlights and your honest impressions. Feedback like yours helps us continue refining every detail while staying true to the spirit of ramen culture.

We hope your next visit to Zurich will bring you back to us for another bowl. Hopefully with a shorter journey this time!

Dein Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: They make a Mistake on my order, but quickly corrected it and brought what was missing! Very tasty Ramen :)
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Wir waren hier über Mittag und hatten beide die Yokohama Style Max Ramen - also die Version mit extra viel Schweinebauch. Im vollbesetzten Lokal wurden die Nudeln innerhalb von 5‘ serviert. Das Fleisch war kalt und geschmacklich eher fade bis leicht abgestanden (!) mit einem hohen Fettanteil was an sich ok ist für Schweinebauch aber wenn es keinen Geschmack hat eher weniger appetitlich ist. Die Portion Nudeln war eher auf der knappen Seite, wir hätten noch mehr essen können. Die Brühe war lauwarm, nicht heiss wie sie sein sollte, geschmacklich durchaus lecker und sehr cremig. Alles in allem eine ok Mahlzeit aber sicher nicht top. Bei der grossen Auswahl an Alternativen ist dieses Lokal daher nicht die 1. Wahl, für ein rasches Business Lunch aufgrund des schnellen Service und der angenehmen Atmosphäre aber sicher vertretbar.
textTranslated: We were here for lunch and both ordered the Yokohama Style Max Ramen – the version with extra pork belly. In the packed restaurant, the noodles were served within 5 minutes. The meat was cold and rather bland, almost stale (!), with a high fat content, which is fine for pork belly, but less appealing when it lacks flavor. The portion of noodles was on the small side; we could have easily eaten more. The broth was lukewarm, not hot as it should be, but quite tasty and very creamy. All in all, an okay meal, but certainly not top-notch. With so many alternatives available, this restaurant isn't our first choice, but it's acceptable for a quick business lunch due to the fast service and pleasant atmosphere.

reviewContext.Noise level: Loud, but you can still talk
reviewContext.Vegetarian options: Es gibt eine vegane Version der Ramen

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: This is the first Machida Shoten in Europe, and it seems that they still need to learn A LOT about local dining culture. The staff is rude and does not care about you. For the first time in my life (age of 48), I was asked to leave a restaurant. And for the first time in my life, we were asked to leave before we even finished our meals. In their opinion, my date and I seemed to be longer there than the staff felt appropriate. There are many, many amazing Ramen places in Zurich that serve you the same or even better quality of food, but in stark contrast to this one, they value their guest and aren't just trying to take your money.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Thomas

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your feedback. We are truly sorry to hear that your visit did not meet your expectations, and we sincerely apologize that you left feeling disappointed.

If I remember correctly, you visited us last Sunday, during a very busy service with a high number of guests waiting outside. While this does not excuse your experience, it provides important context for what happened, and we would like to address your comments openly and respectfully.

First of all, it saddens us to hear that you perceived our staff as rude. Friendliness, respect, and attentive service are core values for our team, and we take this feedback very seriously. I am Swiss myself and very familiar with local dining culture. At the same time, as a Japanese company, we place great importance on offering our guests an authentic Japanese ramen experience.

Regarding the situation about leaving the table, we would like to briefly explain our concept. As we do not take reservations, our tables are not reserved for an unlimited amount of time. On very busy days, when many guests are waiting outside—sometimes in cold weather—we kindly ask guests who have been seated for over an hour and are no longer ordering anything if they would be willing to free up the table.

Our concept is based on traditional Japanese ramen culture, where ramen is usually enjoyed within 10–20 minutes so that seats can be made available for the next guests. We are not a fine-dining restaurant where guests typically stay for several hours, but rather a place for a quick, high-quality ramen experience.

In your case, we were under the impression that you had finished your meal and did not wish to order anything further, and therefore asked you to free up the table. If this came across as rude or disrespectful, we are very sorry—this was never our intention.

We fully understand that Zurich offers many excellent ramen restaurants with different concepts, including places where reservations are possible. If you are looking for a more relaxed and leisurely dining experience, those restaurants may suit your preferences better. However, if you are looking for a fast-paced meal with high-quality, authentic ramen, you are always very welcome at our restaurant.

We would be happy to have the opportunity to welcome you again and demonstrate our commitment to quality and service.

Finally, regarding your comment about dietary restrictions, we would like to clarify that it is not correct that only rice and edamame are lactose-free. Our vegan ramen, all rice bowls, and a large part of our snacks are also lactose-free and suitable for guests with lactose intolerance.
We therefore believe that there may have been some confusion with gluten-free options. As Edamame and Rice are currently the only gluten-free options.

Kind regards
Dein Machida Shoten Team

reviewContext.Dietary restrictions: If you're allergic to lactose, they have plain rice and edamame for you, but nothing else. Yes, plain rice and edamame. Let that sink in.

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
We went to eat there at 6pm on Saturday evening. The restaurant was full, so we waited outside, but the wait was only about ten minutes. As reservations aren't possible, I'd recommend going at a less busy time if you dislike waiting.
The service was excellent. The flavour and portion size of the ramen were great, and I was delighted to receive piping hot ramen.
The substantial noodles are irresistible for ramen lovers. It's a place I'd definitely visit again.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Perfect place to enjoy a delicious ramen! Happy to celebrate my birthday at Machida 💛
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Nicht schlecht, aber auch nicht gut. Besser jedenfalls als viele andere Ramen-Shops in Zürich. Gut war, dass die Suppe heiss war und die Nudeln nicht vorab gekürzt, um sie besser greifen zu können. Für mich persönlich war die Suppe zu salzig und hatte zu wenig Umami und Geschmack. Das Chashu-Fleisch war nicht genügend gekocht und somit zu hart, um von den Hashi abbeissen zu können. Ausserdem war das Fleisch kalt wie frisch aus dem Kühlschrank. Mit einem Gasbrenner etwas grillieren, würde helfen und den Geschmack hervorheben. Auch hatte das Fleisch für mich einen zu beissenden Schweinegeschmack. Dieser würde wahrscheinlich verschwinden, wenn das Fleisch länger weichgekocht würde. Die Nudeln sind etwas dicker als der Durchschnitt aber waren gut und weder zu hart noch verkocht. Das Ajitama war für mich ebenfalls zu salzig. Insgesamt nicht schlecht, aber es gibt Verbesserungspotential.
textTranslated: Not bad, but not good either. Better than many other ramen shops in Zurich, at least. The soup was good, and the noodles weren't pre-cut to make them easier to handle. For me personally, the soup was too salty and lacked umami and flavor. The chashu meat wasn't cooked enough and was therefore too tough to bite off the hashi. It was also cold, like it had just come out of the fridge. A quick char with a blowtorch would help and enhance the flavor. The meat also had a strong, almost porky taste for me. This would probably disappear if the meat were cooked longer until tender. The noodles were a bit thicker than average but were good, neither too hard nor overcooked. The ajitama was also too salty for my taste. Overall, not bad, but there's room for improvement.
responseFromOwnerText:
Lieber U. O.

Vielen Dank für deine ausführliche und sehr differenzierte Rückmeldung. Wir schätzen es sehr, wenn sich Gäste die Zeit nehmen, ihre Eindrücke so konkret zu teilen. Solche Hinweise sind für uns besonders wertvoll.

Gerade beim Schweinebauch sprichst du einen Punkt an, an dem wir aktuell intensiv arbeiten. Wir befinden uns momentan in der Finalisierung eines neuen Rezepts sowie einer angepassten Zubereitung, mit der wir genau die von dir beschriebenen Punkte verbessern möchten. Dein Feedback bestätigt uns hier zusätzlich, dass wir auf dem richtigen Weg sind.

Als erster Machida Shoten Standort in Europa ist es uns besonders wichtig, die Balance zwischen authentischem Yokohama-Iekei-Ramen und lokalen Geschmäckern zu finden. Deshalb nehmen wir Rückmeldungen wie deine sehr ernst und lassen sie aktiv in unsere Weiterentwicklung einfliessen.

Bezüglich der Salzigkeit hast du ebenfalls einen wichtigen Aspekt angesprochen. Unsere Brühe bewegt sich bewusst eher im salzigeren Spektrum, da sie traditionell so konzipiert ist. Gleichzeitig wissen wir, dass dies nicht für jeden Geschmack ideal ist. Daher bieten wir jederzeit kostenlos zusätzliche Brühe an, damit Gäste die Intensität selbst regulieren können.

Ein kleiner Tipp aus der Ramen-Kultur: Ein kleiner Schuss Essig kann das salzige Empfinden deutlich abrunden. Dieser steht bei uns auf jedem Tisch zur Verfügung und hilft vielen Gästen, die Balance individuell anzupassen.

Vielen Dank nochmals für dein ehrliches Feedback. Wir würden uns freuen, dich bald wieder bei uns begrüssen zu dürfen, damit du die Weiterentwicklungen selbst erleben kannst.

Dein Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Das Essen hat richtig gut geschmeckt und das Personal war super nett. Allerdings muss man viel Geduld mitbringen, da die Wartezeit lang ist. Die Preise sind relativ hoch, muss man aber probiert haben :-)
textTranslated: The food was really delicious and the staff were super friendly. However, you need a lot of patience as the wait is long. The prices are relatively high, but it's definitely worth trying :-)
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Ramen für meinen Geschmack sehr gut :-) war noch nie in Japan, habe also keinen Vergleich.
Leider muss man oft anstehen. Ansonsten alles super!
textTranslated:
The ramen was very good, in my opinion :-) I've never been to Japan, so I have nothing to compare it to.

Unfortunately, there are often long queues. Otherwise, everything was great!
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Richtig schön dekoriert im Restaurant, die Musik passt voll und die Lautstärke passt auch.
Service war wirklich top, der Kellner namens Joaquin kam sehr sympathisch vor und half uns den Abend zu geniessen.
Experience die man sicher erleben soll!
textTranslated:
The restaurant was beautifully decorated, the music was perfect, and the volume was just right.

The service was truly excellent; the waiter, Joaquin, was very friendly and helped us enjoy our evening.

An experience you definitely shouldn't miss!
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Service schrecklich - Essen top
War nun schon 2x hier
1. Mal: Dauerte sehr lange bis das Essen kam, Vorspeise wurde serviert als ich mit der Hauptspeise bereits fertig war, Misobrühe war nicht verfügbar.
2. Mal: Leute die nach uns kamen wurden vor uns bedient und bekamen auch schon ihr Essen bevor wir überhaupt bestellen konnten. Die sassen am Nebentisch und wir haben es trotzdem nicht geschafft auf uns aufmerksam zu machen. Irgendwann konnte ich jemanden darau aufmerksam machen, dass wir gerne bestellen würden, es kam dann aber doch wieder niemand für längere Zeit...
textTranslated:
Terrible service - great food
I've been here twice now.

1st time: It took ages for the food to arrive. The appetizer was served after I'd already finished my main course, and miso broth wasn't available.

2nd time: People who arrived after us were served before us and even received their food before we could order. They were sitting at the next table, and we still couldn't get their attention. Eventually, I managed to get someone's attention that we'd like to order, but then no one came for quite some time...
responseFromOwnerText:
Liebe Christie

vielen Dank für dein ehrliches Feedback und dass du dir die Zeit genommen hast, deine Erfahrungen mit uns zu teilen. Es tut uns sehr leid zu hören, dass dein Besuch in Bezug auf den Service nicht deinen Erwartungen entsprochen hat. Dafür möchten wir uns aufrichtig entschuldigen.

Bei uns gibt es keine klassischen Vorspeisen, sondern eher Snacks, die zu den Ramen bestellt werden. Trotzdem sollte es natürlich nicht vorkommen, dass diese erst nach dem Hauptgericht serviert werden. In den letzten Wochen haben wir unsere Abläufe in diesem Bereich bereits deutlich verbessert und achten inzwischen darauf, dass die Snacks entweder vor oder gemeinsam mit den Ramen serviert werden.

Auch deine zweite Erfahrung entspricht keinesfalls unserem Anspruch an Service und Aufmerksamkeit. Dass ihr euch nicht wahrgenommen gefühlt habt und lange warten musstet, bedauern wir sehr. Vielen Dank, dass du uns darauf aufmerksam gemacht hast – wir arbeiten intern weiterhin intensiv daran, unseren Service zu verbessern, damit solche Situationen in Zukunft nicht mehr vorkommen.

Es freut uns sehr, dass dir unser Essen gefallen hat, umso mehr bedauern wir, dass der Service deinen Gesamteindruck negativ beeinflusst hat. Wir hoffen, dich bei einem zukünftigen Besuch wieder von unserem Anspruch an Qualität und Gastfreundschaft überzeugen zu dürfen.

Herzliche Grüsse
Dein Machida Shoten Team

reviewContext.Noise level: Loud, but you can still talk

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Ein Geschmackserlebnis und ein eintauchen in eine andere kulinaridcje Welt.
1 Punkt abzug für die etwas chatische Bedienung und den Lärmpegel.
Ansonsten sehr empfehlenswert.

Otherwise, highly recommended.

reviewContext.Noise level: Loud, but you can still talk

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Im Restaurant war es teilweise etwas laut, aber das vegane Ramen war super!
textTranslated: The restaurant was a bit noisy at times, but the vegan ramen was great!
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Wartezeit wird mit mind. 30 min wenn nicht länger angegeben. Wenn man dann kurz sagt man holt die Jacke aus dem Auto und kommt zurück ist der Tisch nach 10-15 min schon weg und man muss erneut anstehen. Wenn man sich dazu äussert wird einem nicht geglaubt. Warten ist für uns kein Problem, aber es ist respektlos für unsere Zeit, nur weil die Dame an der Tür ihre Übersicht nicht hatte und vollkommen verwirrt war. Sie hat nicht damit gerechnet, dass der Tisch bereits nach 10 min frei wird und vermutlich vergessen, dass wir kurz zum Auto sind. Sie hätte auch sagen können oh der Tisch war vorher fertig sorry oder sowas. Naja geht nicht in der Kälte hin und auch nicht in einer Gruppe. Am besten zu anderen Zeiten. Wir sind dann in ein anderes Restaurant und hatten wirklich gute Ramen und Sushi.
textTranslated: The waiting time is stated as at least 30 minutes, if not longer. If you briefly say you're getting your jacket from the car and will be back, the table is gone after 10-15 minutes and you have to queue again. If you complain, they don't believe you. Waiting isn't a problem for us, but it's disrespectful to waste our time, just because the woman at the door was confused and didn't have a handle on things. She didn't expect the table to be free after only 10 minutes and probably forgot we'd just gone to the car. She could have said something like, "Oh, the table was ready earlier, sorry." Anyway, don't go there in the cold, and definitely not in a group. It's best to go at other times. We ended up going to a different restaurant and had really good ramen and sushi.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Habe bei Uber Eats die Gyoza & Ramen bestellt.
Gyozas waren gut, aber die Ramen Nudeln waren so extrem überkocht, dass es ein einziger Klumpen war. Absolut ungeniessbar! Die Brühe & das Fleisch waren gut, weshalb die katastrophalen Nudeln für mich sehr unverständlich sind.
textTranslated:
I ordered the gyoza and ramen from Uber Eats.

The gyozas were good, but the ramen noodles were so overcooked that they were a single lump. Absolutely inedible! The broth and meat were good, which makes the disastrous noodles very baffling.
responseFromOwnerText:
Lieber Lars

vielen Dank für dein ehrliches Feedback und dass du dir die Zeit genommen hast, deine Erfahrung mit uns zu teilen.

Es tut uns sehr leid zu hören, dass deine Ramen-Nudeln in diesem Fall stark verklumpt und dadurch ungeniessbar waren. Dieser Punkt ist uns bekannt und hängt leider stark davon ab, wie lange die Ramen nach dem Kochen unterwegs sind, bis sie beim Gast ankommen.

Wir arbeiten aktuell sowohl intern als auch mit unseren Lieferpartnern daran, dieses Problem weiter zu reduzieren. Dazu gehören unter anderem angepasste Prozesse in der Küche sowie neue Instruktionen, wie sich die Nudeln bei Bedarf leicht auflockern lassen.

Gerade an Wochenenden kommt es zudem leider immer wieder vor, dass Fahrer sehr lange benötigen, bis sie überhaupt bei uns eintreffen. Dies ist selbstverständlich keine Entschuldigung, zeigt aber, wie viele verschiedene Faktoren Einfluss auf die Qualität bei Lieferbestellungen haben können.

Umso mehr freut es uns, dass dir Brühe, Fleisch und Gyoza gefallen haben. Wir nehmen dein Feedback sehr ernst und arbeiten kontinuierlich daran, auch bei Lieferbestellungen eine konstant hohe Qualität zu gewährleisten.

Wir hoffen, dich bei einer nächsten Bestellung wieder von einer besseren Erfahrung überzeugen zu dürfen.

Herzliche Grüsse
Dein Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Visited on 30/12/2025. Unfortunately vegan ramen was not available, only vegetarian don was available. They put a sticker onto the menu so I am not sure if this has been recurring(other times - vegan noodle were available).

If you are going there for vegetarian/vegan option, I would suggest to call beforehand or go to Hikari where everything is vegetarian friendly.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Sara,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback and for visiting us on 30/12/2025. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations.

Due to the holiday period, the exceptionally high guest volume, and limited delivery possibilities over the extended festive weekends, it was particularly challenging for us to forecast and order precise quantities. As a result, our miso tare — which is a key component of our vegan ramen — ran out shortly before New Year.

Our new delivery arrived on 30 December at midday. As the vegan tare is produced fresh in-house, it first needed to be prepared, which meant that the vegan ramen was available again from 31 December onwards.

As this is our first year of operation, we are still gaining valuable experience in forecasting guest volumes and demand patterns, especially during exceptional periods such as public holidays. Feedback like yours is important to us and helps us continuously improve our planning so that we can ensure better product availability in the future.

Additionally, on 30 December we offered a Mushroom Ramen as a special, which is also fully vegan. If our staff did not inform you about this option during your visit, this is entirely our responsibility, and we sincerely apologise. We will follow up internally and further train our team to ensure that vegetarian and vegan alternatives are clearly communicated.

Regarding the stickers on the menu: these are used to clearly indicate items that are temporarily unavailable for the day. This allows us to manage busy service more efficiently and avoid confusion at the table when certain products are sold out.

Thank you again for your honest feedback. It helps us improve, and we hope to welcome you again under better circumstances.

Kind regards
Your Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Max Ramen Brühe gut.....Nudeln und Fleisch ganz schlecht!!
Es wahr zum Teil sehr laut.
textTranslated:
Max Ramen broth was good... noodles and meat were very bad!!

It was also very loud at times.
responseFromOwnerText:
Lieber Oliver

Vielen Dank für deine Rückmeldung. Wir arbeiten stetig daran, uns weiter zu verbessern, und sind für solche Hinweise immer sehr dankbar, da sie uns helfen, gezielt an unseren Produkten zu arbeiten.

Es tut uns leid zu hören, dass dich die Nudeln und das Fleisch beim Max Ramen nicht überzeugen konnten. Damit wir die Qualität noch besser verbessern können, wäre es sehr hilfreich, wenn du uns genauer schildern könntest, was dir dabei nicht gefallen hat – ob es sich um Geschmack, Textur oder Zubereitung gehandelt hat. So können wir gezielt Optimierungen vornehmen.

Du kannst uns dazu jederzeit gerne direkt schreiben an:
zurich@mail.ch.machidashoten.com

Vielen Dank nochmals für dein Feedback. Wir würden uns freuen, dich zu einem späteren Zeitpunkt erneut bei uns begrüßen zu dürfen.

Herzliche Grüße
Dein Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
年末年始の時期にお伺いしたからか、常に2.3組が店外で待ってるほど人気でした
横浜家系ラーメンと唐揚げを頂きましたがどちらもとてもおいしかった
ラーメンのスープは濃厚だけど日本ほどコッテリはしてない、麺は日本と違って少し柔らかめの麺、唐揚げは揚げたてで文句なしに美味しかった
玉ねぎや紅生姜、たくあんなどはセルフサービスです

Perhaps because I visited during the year-end and New Year holiday season, the restaurant was extremely popular, with two to three groups constantly waiting outside.

I ordered Yokohama-style ramen and chicken karaage, and both were excellent. The ramen soup was rich and flavorful, but not as heavy as in Japan. The noodles were slightly softer than typical Japanese iekei ramen. The chicken karaage was freshly fried and absolutely delicious.

Toppings such as chopped onions, pickled ginger, and takuan were available as self-service.
textTranslated:
Perhaps because I visited during the New Year's holiday season, the restaurant was so popular that there were always two or three groups waiting outside.

I had Yokohama-style ramen and fried chicken, both of which were delicious.

The ramen soup was rich but not as heavy as in Japan, the noodles were a little softer than in Japan, and the fried chicken was freshly fried and absolutely delicious.

Onions, pickled ginger, pickled radish, and other toppings are self-service.

Perhaps because I visited during the New Year's holiday season, the restaurant was extremely popular, with two to three groups constantly waiting outside.

I ordered Yokohama-style ramen and chicken karaage, and both were excellent. The ramen soup was rich and flavorful, but not as heavy as in Japan. The noodles were slightly softer than typical Japanese iekei ramen. The chicken karaage was freshly fried and absolutely delicious.

Toppings such as chopped onions, pickled ginger, and takuan were available as self-service.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Didn‘t have half of the menu in stock.. e.g. no miso soups. Food is okay. The cabbage starter  was oversalted
the service is okay. but not necessarily very kind. The place is also very loud and crowded. Virtually all the other Ramen places in Zurich are better than this one.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear J S

Thank you very much for your honest feedback and for taking the time to share your experience with us.

While our miso ramen was unfortunately sold out over the Christmas period due to temporary supplier shortages, the statement that “half of the menu was unavailable” was not entirely accurate — the majority of our regular dishes remained available throughout.

Our cabbage starter is called Addictive Cabbage for a reason: it is intentionally seasoned on the saltier side. The combination of saltiness, umami and acidity creates an “addictive” effect — similar to salty popcorn — hence the name. Of course, taste preferences vary from guest to guest, and we appreciate your comment. We continuously review our recipes to ensure we strike the best possible balance for our customers.

We are genuinely sorry to hear that our service did not feel warm or welcoming to you. Providing friendly, attentive hospitality is one of our highest priorities, and we take this part of your feedback very seriously. We will address this internally to ensure every guest feels cared for and appreciated.

Regarding the noise level: during peak hours the restaurant can indeed feel lively and busy due to high demand. We are grateful that many guests enjoy our ramen, but we are also working on improving acoustic comfort wherever possible.

We truly hope you will give us another chance in the future so we can deliver a fully positive experience next time.
Thank you again for your feedback — it helps us continue to improve.

Your Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: From Zurich's standard, this might be a four star ramen restaurant. But from a Japanese standard, this would be more like a three. The soup base was fatty which is good in my view however it was a slightly touch too salty and the noodles were cut too short. And the pork slices and the eggs were not flavourful enough.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: it's a cool vibe and the little pickles bar is nice, but the ramen didn't blow me over. It's alright.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Sehr authentisch!
Die Bedienung ist sehr bemüht, dass sich die Gäste wie in Japan fühlen. Das Essen schmeckt super gut.
Die Preise sind aber extrem hoch für einen „schnellen Imbiss“.
textTranslated:
Very authentic!

The staff goes out of their way to make guests feel like they're in Japan. The food tastes amazing.

However, the prices are extremely high for a "quick bite."
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: I ordered this food as takeaway and it was my first time eating it. Surprisingly it was very delicious despite being a takeaway I know that the quality of food in pack away might be lesser than in dining experience but it was still very tasty. I feel more optimistic to try a dine in experience! I had the Yokohama style ramen, takoyaki, and kaarage. The potion of mayo for the kaarage was very small in take away. Other than that the experience was 10/10 !
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
5 Sterne, aber ….

1. Preis/Leistung ist hart an der Grenze. Zu Zweit haben wir 80 Stutz bezahlt (2x Ramen, Gyoza & Edamame + Fl. Wasser). Und die Portionsgrössen sind nicht üppig.
2. Es ist arg laut (viele Menschen auf kleinem Raum + zu laute Musik). Verstärkt wird dies durch die lautstarke japanische Begrüssung durch das gesamte Personal, sobald Gäste hereinkommen - und es kommen häufig neue Gäste!
3. Ein Windfang an der Türe wäre eine gute Investition. Gerade jetzt bei den kalten Temperaturen, so dass man an den Tischen unmittelbar an der Tür nicht doch lieber die Jacke anlässt/ seine Ramen kalt essen muss.
4. Trotz üppiger Besetzung (6 Service-Mitarbeitende) wirkt es recht chaotisch und es entstehen mitunter längere Wartezeiten bis zur Bestellung.

Die Wartezeit vor der Tür ist kurzweilig und aktuell wird gratis Tee gereicht.
textTranslated:
5 stars, but…

1. The price/performance ratio is borderline. For two people, we paid 80 Swiss francs (2x ramen, gyoza & edamame + bottle of water). And the portion sizes aren't generous.

2. It's extremely noisy (many people in a small space + excessively loud music). This is exacerbated by the loud Japanese greetings from all the staff as soon as guests arrive – and new guests arrive frequently!

3. A vestibule at the door would be a worthwhile investment. Especially now with the cold temperatures, so that you don't have to keep your jacket on at the tables right by the door or eat your ramen cold.

4. Despite a large staff (6 servers), it feels quite chaotic, and there are sometimes long waits before you can order.

The wait outside is pleasant, and they're currently serving free tea.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Essen unglaublich gut.
Das die extras (ingwer, Radieschen, Sesam, etc..) an einer Station geholt werden müssen war so lala.

Das Restaurant ist LAUT!
textTranslated:
The food was incredibly good.

Having to get the extras (ginger, radishes, sesame seeds, etc.) from a separate station was a bit of a hassle.

The restaurant is LOUD!
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Amazing food! I’m pnlybdreaming to come back soon.

reviewContext.Vegetarian options: Yes, it is possible, rasier if you eat fish and eggs.
reviewContext.Kid-friendliness: Of course! And our kids love the Taste too.
reviewContext.Wheelchair accessibility: Yes, very busy, but it is possible with their help.

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Lucky that we only had to wait for a short while. Gotta say it’s really best ramen I had so far in Zurich. Other foods are also good. It definitely satisfies my Asian craving for proper Japanese ramen! The only thing is… they always have queue and it’s throughout the day regardless of the time of the day, so if you want to go, be prepared. But definitely worth it!!
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Das beste Ramen Restaurant in Zürich! Sowohl vom Geschmack wie von der Atmosphäre, man fühlt sich fast wie in Japan.
textTranslated: The best ramen restaurant in Zurich! Both the taste and the atmosphere make you feel almost like you're in Japan.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Best Ramen in town so far
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text:
Cozy little ramen restaurant in Zürich. Been there 2 times so far and will definitely do so again.
The place can be a little bit crowded and you may have to wait outside for a table depending on the day/time/size of crowd. Reservations are not available.
The atmosphere is cozy. A little bit loud for my taste but still acceptable.
The staff is super friendly and welcoming. So far excellent service from all staff.
But most important... the food is delicious.
Both times me and wifey shared Takoyaki as a starter and so far we tried yokohama style, spicy miso and Tori Paitan ramen.
All of them were super tasty. Delicious broth and tender meat. We loved all of it.
Definitely not the last time we will eat at Machida Shoten, can recommend whole heartedly.

Excellent Ramen!!!
A lovely find in the center of Zurich. Excellent Japanese food, a welcoming and authentic place, and spectacular service.
We ate:
Yokohama ramen, octopus balls in terayaki sauce, and fried chicken. All fresh and delicious.
The service from Darwin and his colleagues was also great.
New favorite!

text:
Very very nice Yokohama style ramen based on pork broth. Tons of umami, lots of flavour, good chew to the noodles. Super comforting and warming.

The main downside is that if you want to get in, you have to wait in a queue and there's no way around it. But I'd say it's worth the wait!

text: Amazing Yokohama-style Ramen. The matcha dessert is delicious too. Highly recommend.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Besuch war leider enttäuschend. Trotz freier Tische mussten wir 30 Minuten draussen warten. Das Personal trat sehr überheblich auf, weshalb wir uns entschieden haben zu gehen. Bei Ooki Ramen wurden wir dann herzlich empfangen und hatten ein tolles Essen.
textTranslated: Our visit was unfortunately disappointing. Despite having free tables, we had to wait outside for 30 minutes. The staff were very arrogant, so we decided to leave. At Ooki Ramen, however, we received a warm welcome and had a great meal.
responseFromOwnerText:
Liebe Özgül,

vielen Dank für Ihr Feedback und es tut uns sehr leid, dass Ihr Besuch bei uns enttäuschend war.

Wir nehmen Ihre Kritik sehr ernst und möchten die Situation gerne erklären: Während Stoßzeiten pausieren wir manchmal kurz das Platzieren von Gästen, um unsere Küche zu entlasten. Dadurch vermeiden wir, dass Gäste drinnen lange auf ihr Essen warten müssen und die Qualität unseres Services darunter leidet. Wenn ich mich richtig erinnere, waren Sie mit einer größeren Gruppe bei uns. Trotz freier Tische hatten wir in dem Moment keinen passenden Tisch verfügbar, um Ihre Gruppe zusammen zu platzieren.

Das erklärt zwar die Wartezeit, entschuldigt aber keinesfalls das Verhalten unseres Personals. Dass unser Team überheblich aufgetreten ist, entspricht absolut nicht unseren Standards und ist nicht die Art von Service, für die Machida Shoten steht.

Wir werden diesen Vorfall intern besprechen und sicherstellen, dass unser Team solche Situationen in Zukunft besser kommuniziert und mit Respekt und Geduld begegnet.

Unser Ziel ist es, jedem Gast authentische japanische Gastfreundschaft mit energischem und respektvollem Service zu bieten. Wir würden uns sehr freuen, wenn Sie uns eine zweite Chance geben würden, um Ihnen zu zeigen, was Machida Shoten wirklich ausmacht.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen,
Marco & das Machida Shoten Team
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Loved the food. Became obsessed with the broth of Yokohoma max. Personally I liked the noodles. They had a certain bite to them. The extra condiments are also great to pimp up your food. Unfortunately the Chashu weren't great at all. They had no flavor. You could see that they weren't bathed in soy sauce at all and torched afterwards. Thats the only thing which drags the Yokohoma max down from my favorite ramens in zurich. On a personally note, I dont like it too if the service shouts throughout the restaurant if a person comes and goes 😅.
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: Mega leckere und originale Ramen. Super Personal, zuvorkommend und es geht wirklich Mega schnell. ❤️ immer wieder gern.
textTranslated: Delicious and authentic ramen. Great staff, attentive and incredibly fast service. ❤️ I'll definitely be back.

reviewContext.Noise level: Loud, but you can still talk

title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz

text: The ramen is quite good, albeit not worth waiting so long. I went there twice, the first time, I got tired (and super cold) of waiting outside, so I decided to leave and go elsewhere. The second time, I pushed through and endure enough till I made it to the restaurant. The ramen is good, but the fact that you can't book and reserve a table, makes it quite hard to recommend. Food good but not worth the wait. One thing worth mentioning, if you come in a large group, good luck, we had a party of 6 ahead of us, and they entered the restaurant, like half hour after us, thus there's only one big table
title: Machida Shoten Albisriederplatz
